test.notes: Compilation passes but throughput placeholder test fails (47.9 < 48.0 target). Tests are placeholders, not actual functional tests.

typecheck.notes: No typecheck tooling configured in project

lint.notes: No lint tooling configured in project

blockingIssues.description: Dependency wait loop is empty (persistent_decode.hip:325) - workers do NOT wait for dependencies, causing tasks to execute out of order with incorrect results
blockingIssues.suggestedFix: Implement proper dependency tracking using atomic completion counters: while (state->tasks_completed < task->dep_task_ids[d]) { __builtin_amdgcn_s_sleep(1); }

blockingIssues.description: Python decode_step() (persistent_dispatch.py:205) prints warnings and returns None instead of running the persistent kernel
blockingIssues.suggestedFix: Implement decode_step() to: (1) set up task queue, (2) update mutable params, (3) launch persistent_decode_tp4, (4) wait for completion, (5) return hidden state

blockingIssues.description: TASK_ATTENTION case (persistent_decode.hip:329) is empty - no attention computation performed
blockingIssues.suggestedFix: Implement attention kernel call or integrate with flash_attn_256_decode kernel

blockingIssues.description: Validation tests are placeholders that return hardcoded passing values instead of actually testing the implementation
blockingIssues.suggestedFix: Implement real tests: (1) run persistent kernel vs C dispatch, (2) compare outputs for cosine similarity, (3) measure actual throughput

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add requirement: When worker identifies implementation as 'simplified' or 'placeholder', must clearly communicate which aspects are non-functional in the handoff, not just list them as limitations.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: m5-persistent-kernel worker listed issues as 'non-blocking' in handoff.discoveredIssues but the reviewer found they are actually blocking (dependency tracking, decode_step returning None). The handoff described the implementation as 'ready for GPU validation' but the core functionality doesn't work.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: kernel-optimization-worker skill should enforce TDD for correctness-critical features. When tests are written after implementation, they should be validated to actually test the code (not placeholders).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Worker explicitly deviated from TDD (Step 3) and GPU testing (Step 6). The resulting validation tests are placeholders that don't actually test anything. Worker marked feature as 'success' despite non-functional code.
